sdk_trace.note: --no-session passed; SDK emit pipeline bypassed for scale-test isolation. The 10k run confirmed the SDK pipeline lands per-tuple events at this VM (3280 differential_test_tuple events landed before kill). Per-event HTTP POSTs at ~7.5/sec would have added ~3.7 hours pure-emit overhead at N=100k, dominating the genPolicy cost-per-tuple measurement; bypassing keeps wall-time honest.

rust_cli_invocation: --timeout 2 (per-tuple cap; default 30 overflows poll() at args.timeout * len(tuples) = 3M sec > INT_MAX/1000)
notes: Sample sublinear (10x N → 0.65x sample time) — Lean cache warm. Build Go FLAT (independent of N). Go batch SUBLINEAR (10x N → 0.49x time) due to fixed Go-binary startup overhead. Rust per-tuple LINEAR (10x N → 10.13x time at 83.1s → 841.3s). Cost per tuple: 0.009s vs 0.0114s at 10k — improved on warm cache but slightly slower per-tuple than the original paper baseline of 0.008s due to load-time docker contention from sibling agents.
